lenses.guidance: Esau running and embracing (v 4) inverts the deception of gen 27 — the brother Jacob feared most becomes the brother who weeps on his neck. The narrative structure makes reconciliation the climactic reversal of the cycle.

Twenty years of fear collapse in v 4's embrace. The covenant promise of land and seed (28:13-15) advances by way of unmerited reconciliation with the wronged brother — grace, not strategy, returns Jacob to Canaan.

Esau runs to embrace the deceiver (v 4), foreshadowing the father in Luke 15:20 who runs to the prodigal — and behind it, Christ who reconciles by absorbing the wrong rather than collecting it. Mercy outruns merit.

'I have seen your face as one sees the face of God' (v 10) — Jacob reads the night wrestling and Esau's mercy as the same grace. Worship today by naming where God has met you in the face of an unexpected reconciliation.
